descriptions.text: これは、タイトーの横スクロール型ビート 'em upの移植版で、後に非公式の続編「ターゲット: レネゲード」と「レネゲード III: ザ・ファイナルチャプター」が制作されました。ゲームはブルックリンの厳しい街並みを舞台にしており、彼女に会うために冒険し、誘拐犯から彼女を救出する必要があります。これは5つのレベルに分かれており、地下鉄や港、さらには厳しい街を通り抜けます。頭突き、キック、パンチ、飛び蹴りなど、さまざまな攻撃的な動きが用意されています。ゲームの暴力表現は、かなり軽妙なスタイルで描かれており、ユーモアの要素も含まれています。

Nekketsu Kōha Kunio-kun, released as Renegade in the West, is a 1986 beat 'em up video game developed by Technōs Japan and published by Taito for arcades. In the original Japanese version, the game revolves around Kunio, a high school delinquent who must stand up against a series of rival gangs that are targeting his classmate Hiroshi. In the Western version, the player controls a street brawler who must face four different gangs in order to rescue his girlfriend, who is being held captive by a mob boss.
Conceptualized and designed by Yoshihisa Kishimoto, the game was semi-autobiographical, partly based on his own teenage high school years getting into daily fights, with Kunio partly based on himself. He also drew inspiration from the Bruce Lee martial arts film Enter the Dragon (1973), which inspired the game's "knock-down-drag-out" fights, along with his own altercations as a youth. In order to make the game more appealing for the West, Technos produced a graphically-altered version with a visual style inspired by the 1979 film The Warriors, changing the looks of some of the game's characters and scenery.
